macro_summary: Monday's US session saw the S&P 500 edge up 0.1% and the Nasdaq 100 add 0.2%, but the VIX surged 16.5% to 20.13 as a tech sell-off resumed on AI spending fears and a hawkish Fed outlook after Bank of America called for three rate hikes this year. Oil steadied near $73.59 following the prior week's 23% monthly collapse, though Iran's military declaration of a Strait of Hormuz closure over the weekend kept geopolitical risk elevated. In Asia, a sharp KOSPI selloff hit SK Hynix (-12.5%) and Samsung, and US pre-market futures point to further tech weakness.
commodities_note: WTI inched 0.1% lower to $73.59, down 8.9% for the week and 23.6% for the month as the Hormuz trade de-escalated, though the Iran-IRGC closure threat injects fresh uncertainty. Gold fell 0.65% to $4,135, pressured by hawkish Fed bets, while the DXY firmed 0.29% to 101.29 as rate-hike expectations lifted the dollar.

R:R 1.1:1 with no defined catalyst; avoid until quarterly orders show sustained optoelectronics momentum.

writeup.headline: CAUTION — 57x PE, defense AI spending offset by unsustainable valuation.
writeup.what_to_do: PLTR's defense AI contracts (Army $10B IDIQ framework, Navy $448M) are strong bookings, but the 57x forward PE and the 141% premium to BigTech peers require a growth acceleration that hasn't materialized. The Zeta Global partnership is incremental, not a thesis-changer. Avoid until the multiple compresses to a justifiable level or revenue growth re-accelerates.

writeup.thesis_break_condition: Thesis breaks if multiple compresses toward the BigTech cluster median (23.8x) without a corresponding earnings re-rating.

singapore.summary: All three Singapore banks remain hard-blocked on technical extension as US10Y clings to 4.49%. The cluster thesis — NIM trough and eventual rate relief — is structurally sound, but the entry gate remains closed. UOB's 22% discount to the cluster median is the lone bright spot, yet no name is actionable until either US10Y breaks below 4.20% or a pullback to the 50-day averages occurs.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally intact, tactically extended — no entry until the US10Y catalyst clears.
singapore.key_development: Singapore May CPI held at 1.8%, below expectations, but the MAS has already tightened; no NIM relief likely until the October review.

semiconductors.summary: The Semis cluster is deeply extended — 11 of 15 watchlist names are hard-blocked above their 50-day SMAs, with SOXX up 10% in 5 sessions before the Monday pause. The premarket selloff across AMD (-7.7%), MU (-9.6%), and AVGO (-4.1%) signals a risk-off rotation as AI capex sustainability debates intensify. The structural AI demand thesis remains intact, but the cluster needs a consolidation or an MU earnings surprise to re-price the risk.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically extended — MU earnings on Wednesday is the near-term pivot.
semiconductors.key_development: MU hits +62% extension ahead of its Q3 print; SK Hynix -12.5% on KOSPI selloff adds to cluster anxiety.

big_tech.summary: BigTech is in flux — MSFT and GOOG have been beaten down to oversold levels, creating rare ACCUMULATE setups in names that were hard-blocked for months. MSFT's Azure concentration risk is the overhang, but at RSI 31 and an R:R of 3.8:1, the risk/reward has flipped. AMZN holds the middle ground, while PLTR's defense wins are overwhelmed by a 141% premium to the cluster. The divergence: the AI spending selloff is opening entry windows that didn't exist last week.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — the selloff has reset MSFT/GOOG entry points, but the earnings binary looms.
big_tech.key_development: MSFT upgraded to ACCUMULATE as the selloff pushes RSI to 31; GOOG's TPU thesis holds despite developer migration headlines.

interconnected.name: Coherent Corp.
interconnected.reason: Receives $2B NVIDIA investment for AI photonics alongside LITE — co-packaged optics for GPU clusters is the same thesis. Any NVDA supply-chain signal directly affects COHR revenue.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if LITE's Greensboro fab announcement or NVDA photonics commentary confirms co-packaged optics volume ramp above $500M annual run-rate.

interconnected.name: Samsung Electronics
interconnected.reason: Direct HBM competitor to SK Hynix — Samsung's HBM3e qualification struggles tighten the memory duopoly, but its foundry and capex scale make it the wildcard in the HBM supply chain.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if Samsung confirms HBM4 qualification by Q3 2026 or wins a major hyperscaler order away from SK Hynix.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Custom ASIC and data-center networking play — directly competes with AVGO in the AI custom-silicon race. Hyperscaler chip diversification (Meta, Google, Amazon) benefits MRVL's order book.
interconnected.entry_note: Becomes interesting if AVGO's Google TPU deal volume disappoints or MRVL announces a custom ASIC win at a hyperscaler beyond its existing base.

active_risks: US-Iran Hormuz tensions: Iran's military declared the Strait of Hormuz closed over a perceived MOU breach, contradicting diplomatic progress; oil held near $74 but full-blockade risk resurfaced.
US-China tech retaliation: China restricted exports to 10 US defense firms in response to the Pentagon's Chinese Military Companies list, escalating beyond the prior semiconductor blacklist.
Fed rate hike path: Bank of America reversed its call to three rate hikes in 2026 as inflation worsens; markets now price a 90% probability of a hike, repricing tech valuations.
AI capex sustainability concerns: Tech megacaps slid as investors questioned hyperscaler AI spending ROI; SpaceX debt sale and CoreWeave insider selling reflect cash-flow stress in the AI buildout.
geopolitical.market_impact: The hawkish Fed outlook lifted DXY and pushed VIX to 20.13, while the semis cluster is extended but fragile — SK Hynix -12.5% and broad premarket drops signal that AI momentum may be peaking. Singapore banks remain range-bound as US10Y stubbornly holds above 4.20%, and the Hormuz whipsaw keeps oil traders on edge.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Iran's military declared the Strait of Hormuz closed, directly contradicting last week's US-Iran peace deal; China announced new export restrictions on US defense firms; Bank of America reversed its Fed outlook to three rate hikes.

base.description: Hormuz stalemate holds, oil stays near $75, the Fed proceeds with one rate hike by year-end. The semis cluster corrects 10–15% but AI fundamentals remain intact, creating entry opportunities in MSFT and GOOG at deeply oversold levels. Singapore banks stay range-bound until US10Y breaks.

optimistic.description: The peace deal is confirmed and Hormuz fully reopens; WTI falls below $70, inflation eases, and the Fed pauses. A tech rebound lifts NVDA back above its 50-day and drives a sharp Asia risk-on rally, easing NIM pressure on Singapore banks.

pessimistic.description: Hormuz closure persists, oil spikes above $90, inflation reaccelerates, and the Fed hikes aggressively. The semis cluster selloff deepens, dragging down Singapore banks with global recession fears as hyperscaler capex is reined in.

wildcard.description: A major AI architecture breakthrough renders current GPU-heavy capex partially obsolete, causing a sudden re-rating of the entire semiconductor supply chain — NVDA, MU, and ASML would suffer the most severe repricing.

geopolitical.portfolio_action: Maintain a defensive posture — no new entry until VIX settles below 18 and the semis cluster consolidates from extreme extensions; selectively accumulate oversold BigTech (MSFT/GOOG) with tight stops.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Cautiously defensive with selective accumulation in oversold BigTech
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 15 of 28 names are hard-blocked on technical extension, and the VIX spike to 20.13 on hawkish Fed bets plus renewed Hormuz uncertainty keeps new entries limited to the two BigTech ACCUMULATE setups.

taxonomy_discrimination.note: Output-only meta-analysis of signal-class discrimination. NOT injected into the analysis prompt and NOT persisted into memory.lessons_learned. full_corpus pools pre-cutover (different-instrument) rows — informational only.

calibration_insights.confidence_banner: Actionable buckets BUY (n=0) / ACCUMULATE (n=6) spanning trend_up. NOT yet decision-grade — alpha figures are benchmark-relative; single-regime samples carry no out-of-regime evidence.
calibration_insights.note: Block is output-only — assembled post-LLM from signal_evaluation_log outcomes and never injected into the analysis prompt. The underlying lesson stats are separately persisted into report_memory.json under lessons_learned and DO reach the next day's prompt via the memory Layer 3 calibration loop. Treat as a reader-facing self-knowledge summary.
